items.prompt: compares, composes, and selects advanced-function families in bounded modeling and representation tasks and interprets near-limit behavior
items.expected_evidence: choose and justify an appropriate advanced-function family or composition for a bounded modeling or interpretation task
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner selects or combines function families in a way that preserves the structure of the task and the resulting behavior.

items.prompt: explains how transformed, inverse, and composed functions interact and how approaching behavior can still be reasoned about before formal calculus
items.expected_evidence: explain approaching behavior and continuity in bounded near-limit situations without relying on formal calculus procedures
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains synthesis and near-limit reasoning as coherent function behavior rather than isolated rules.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to composition_representation_breakdown.
items.expected_evidence: compare transformed, composed, or inverse functions across algebraic and graphical representations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where a composition or transformation was represented inconsistently across forms.

items.prompt: interprets a less-familiar precalculus modeling or graph-comparison context and identifies the dominant cross-family features that matter
items.expected_evidence: connect cross-family features such as growth, asymptotes, oscillation, and local behavior in one coherent analysis
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves coherent cross-family reasoning and identifies the feature that actually governs the situation.
